account_quota_at_cap: You have reached the hard cap (Starter where applicable) or a limit that blocks new runs. Upgrade or wait for the next month.
account_quota_in_overage: Verified runs have exceeded included amount — metered overage applied per plan where enabled; check the Stripe estimate above.
account_quota_notice: You have used at least 75% of your included verifications (account pooled) for this billing month.
account_quota_ok: Usage is comfortably below your included verifications (account pooled) for this billing month.
account_quota_warning: You have used at least 90% of your included verifications, or you are near the top of included before overage billing.
account_quota_zero: No verification usage recorded for this billing month yet.
contracts_terms: Limits and semantics: OpenAPI at /openapi-commercial-v1.yaml (Outcome Certificate v3 with evidenceCompleteness.remediationItems and rerunPath; enforcement ingestion schema_version 3; share envelope schemaVersion 3), plans JSON at /api/v1/commercial/plans, and entitlement docs on GitHub main.
enforcement_terms: Stateful agentskeptic enforce (hosted baseline, drift checks, pinned acceptance) requires a paid plan (not Starter) and the same active subscription and metering model. Optional quick verify lock flags are preview tightening only—not portable lock CI authority.
framework_footnote: CrewAI: supported. LangGraph: supported. AutoGen: removed (not shipped in current Python integration tracks). Any framework able to emit structured tool activity can integrate.
home_commercial_lead: Open-source CLI is unmetered locally. Published npm installs use the commercial build — API key and successful POST /api/v1/usage/reserve apply for licensed CLI runs as described in docs/commercial.md. In-process createDecisionGate stays local and does not call the usage API.
home_mechanism_works_with: Postgres, SQLite, MySQL, MongoDB, S3, HTTP APIs, vector stores, and other queryable systems — full registry contract verification covers witness kinds documented in verification-state-stores.md; quick mode stays SQL-focused.
home_value_proposition: AgentSkeptic compares agent claims with real downstream state — databases, APIs, object stores, and vector stores — and returns a clear verdict. Read-only, deterministic, and CI-friendly.
integrate_requirements_concat: Node.js 22 or newer|Read-only access to at least one queryable store (full contract/registry verification supports SQL, vectors, S3-compatible object storage, HTTP witnesses, Mongo, and SQLite or remote SQL)|Ability to emit or export structured tool activity as NDJSON
local_verify_footnote: Local OSS verification remains free forever.
outcome_certificate_quick_fact: Quick verify is a preview path; contract verification with an Outcome Certificate is what the engine treats as decision-grade for matches.
paid_verification_terms: Starter includes 1,000 published npm CLI verifications per month (hard cap, no overage; account-pooled usage). Individual, Team, and Business include higher monthly amounts plus pay-as-you-go overage; an active subscription is required (trial counts).
pooled_explanation: Monthly licensed verification usage is account-pooled across API keys within the billing month—the same pooling policy described under Quota semantics in docs/commercial.md.
pricing_hero_subtitle_primary: Local and open-source (OSS) paths are unmetered. The published npm CLI with a Starter account includes up to 1,000 licensed verifications per month, account-pooled across API keys within the billing month (hard cap; no overage). Paid self-serve plans add more included usage and metered overage beyond included when subscribed.
pricing_hero_subtitle_secondary: Paid self-serve plans also unlock stateful enforce (baseline, drift, acceptance) and governed CI. Enterprise is contract.
pricing_row_1_fail_ci_oss_verify_label: Fail CI on mismatch, OSS build
pricing_row_2_npm_licensed_label: Published npm CLI + API key, licensed/metered
pricing_row_3_enforce_label: Stateful enforce (baseline, drift, accept), paid only
pricing_row_4_included_monthly_ci_label: Included verified runs, UTC month, pooled per account
quick_sql_only_disclaimer: Quick verification (`agentskeptic quick`) is SQL inference only — full registry contract verification adds vectors, object storage (S3-compatible), HTTP witnesses, Mongo, and relational checks per docs/verification-state-stores.md.
readme_commercial_joined:
**Commercial metering (published npm)** uses `AGENTSKEPTIC_API_KEY` + `POST /api/v1/usage/reserve` as documented in docs/commercial.md — account-pooled quota per billing month.
**OSS/unmetered CLI** for single-run verification: clone this repo and use the OSS build (`WF_BUILD_PROFILE=oss` / default `npm run build` artifact). State over-time `enforce` needs the commercial CLI and a paid entitlement.
security_quick_fact_a: CLI and verification engine run in your infrastructure against databases you configure; the homepage demo runs bundled fixtures on this server for evaluation only.
security_quick_fact_b: Structured tool activity is compared to database query results at verification time; that check does not prove a specific network call caused a row.
security_quick_fact_c: Quick verify is a preview path; contract verification with an Outcome Certificate is what the engine treats as decision-grade for matches.
